description_long: This object is a large, robust concrete structure with three cylindrical arms radiating from a central point, forming a tetrahedral shape. The surface is a uniform, slightly mottled gray, typical of cast concrete, and appears solid and heavy. At the apex of the central arm, there is a visible metal loop or eyelet, likely for lifting or positioning the object with machinery. The ends of each arm are flat and slightly beveled, giving the object a stable resting surface regardless of orientation. The overall design is utilitarian, optimized for stability and resistance to movement, making it suitable for use as a heavy-duty urban barrier or anti-vehicle bollard. There are no visible decorative elements, reflective markings, or openings, emphasizing its functional, industrial character.
description: A heavy, gray concrete bollard with three cylindrical arms and a metal lifting loop at the top, designed for maximum stability and resistance to movement.
description_view_0: Three cylindrical arms are visible, with the metal loop at the top and two arms extending outward at an angle.
description_view_1: The object is seen head-on, showing the symmetrical arrangement of the three arms radiating from the center.
description_view_2: Similar to view 0, but the angle reveals more of the third arm and the metal loop is clearly visible at the apex.
description_view_3: A direct view along one arm, emphasizing the length and flat end of the arm, with the other two arms extending symmetrically to the sides.
